title: Research: coroutine adoption analysis for ensureBackgroundThread refactor
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

Current state: ensureBackgroundThread in FossifyOrg/commons spawns raw Thread().start() for every call (~150 call sites across Calendar alone). No pooling, no structured concurrency, no exception handling.

Desired state: Understanding of feasibility and approach to migrate to coroutine-based threading.

Reason: Research findings from codebase analysis (2026-04-24):

## Scope
- 29 files, ~150 total call sites (ensureBackgroundThread + runOnUiThread)
- Heavy usage: EventsHelper (13), EventActivity (11), TaskActivity (12), MainActivity (8)
- ensureBackgroundThread defined in FossifyOrg/commons helpers/Constants.kt
- Signature: fun ensureBackgroundThread(callback: () -> Unit) — checks isOnMainThread(), spawns Thread if on main, runs inline if not

## Existing Coroutine Usage
- AppStartupWorker already extends CoroutineWorker (work-runtime-ktx dependency exists)
- CalDAVUpdateListener uses Handler+Runnable (separate pattern)
- No raw Thread(), AsyncTask, or Executor usage anywhere

## Threading Patterns
| Pattern | Usage |
|---|---|
| ensureBackgroundThread | All DB/IO work across Activities, Helpers, Dialogs |
| Handler (main thread) | ~10 sites for delayed UI actions, animation, ContentObserver |
| CoroutineWorker | Single AppStartupWorker via WorkManager |

## Refactor Options

### Option A: Internal refactor (commons-only, no signature change)
Replace Thread().start() with CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch inside ensureBackgroundThread. Signature unchanged, zero call-site changes. Affects all FossifyOrg apps.

### Option B: SupervisorJob for exception isolation (recommended baseline)
Top-level SupervisorJob + Dispatchers.IO scope in commons. One callback crashing does not cancel siblings. Add CoroutineExceptionHandler for logging. No per-Activity lifecycle cancellation but no regression vs current Thread approach.

### Option C: Per-component scope (larger refactor, app-level)
Activity/Fragment-scoped SupervisorJob cancelled in onDestroy(). Requires changes in every FossifyOrg app, not just commons.

## Effort Estimate
- Option A/B: 1-2 days (commons PR + version bump)
- Full coroutine adoption (all call sites to suspend/withContext): 2-3 weeks
- Testing: PR #1074 (testing infra) not yet landed — risk factor

## Recommendation
Option B as baseline. SupervisorJob in commons is a safe, backward-compatible improvement. Lifecycle-scoped cancellation is an app-level follow-up that does not require further commons changes.
